title: United States and Iran exchange attacks and oil soars more than 6%
summary: The military escalation between the two countries causes a strong rebound in crude oil and falls in global stock markets amid fears of an energy and inflation crisis.

title: The chip and artificial intelligence sector drives a bullish wave in Asian and US stock markets
summary: Companies like Nvidia, Broadcom, SK Hynix and Meta lead the gains, while South Korea enters a bear market due to doubts about chipmakers.

title: Central banks signal new rate hikes amid persistent inflation
summary: The Fed minutes show division but a hawkish lean, while Euribor exceeds 2.7% and Japanese bonds face upward pressure.

title: Asian stock markets mixed with optimism in Japan and caution in Thailand; Wall Street closes lower
summary: Global markets show divergent trends: the Nikkei rises, the Dow Jones falls, and investors closely watch for signs of a bubble.

title: UniCredit raises its stake in Commerzbank to 48% amid merger battle
summary: The Italian bank, backed by Mutuactivos, strengthens its position in the face of German resistance and brings a possible cross-border deal closer.

title: China's inflation rises 1% in June, below expectations, and drags on Asian stock markets
summary: The CPI data, lower than forecasts, fuels doubts about the recovery of domestic consumption and pressures the Hong Kong and Shanghai exchanges.

title: Bitcoin recovers $60,000 after Warsh comments and Kraken applies for banking license
summary: The crypto market shows mixed signs with bitcoin's recovery, while new regulations and payment applications with stablecoins advance in Spain.

title: Fitch upgrades CaixaBank's rating and Brussels takes Spain to court over capital requirements
summary: The Spanish banking sector faces regulatory challenges while Goldman sees Sabadell's potential exhausted and Santander raises doubts among investors.

title: Costco, Honeywell and PriceSmart report positive second-quarter results
summary: Costco's sales grow in all regions, Honeywell raises its forecasts after the reverse stock split, and PriceSmart improves its revenue.

title: TPG and Blackstone seek more than $4 billion for Hologic unit and Paramount delays merger with Warner
summary: Private equity seeks liquidity, while the Paramount-Warner deal faces regulatory hurdles in Oregon and Indonesia signs a memorandum with Boeing.

title: Europe needs more financial power and conglomerates must reinvent themselves, according to analysts
summary: The continent seeks to strengthen its economic influence, while experts debate the value of private assets for retail investors and the profit bubble.

title: Germany debates rental reform, public pension management and the future of KfW
summary: The country faces regulatory changes in housing and pensions, while the Bundesbank opens the door to managing the capitalization of public income.

title: Japanese companies deploy facial recognition and cybersecurity technologies
summary: Tobu Railway installs facial recognition turnstiles, Rakuten launches services against naval cyberattacks, and e-commerce platforms collaborate against fraud.

title: Japan debates the Imperial Household Law and international pressure over the South China Sea
summary: The opposition party supports the reform of imperial succession, while a Filipino lawyer calls for coordinated pressure against Beijing in maritime arbitration.

title: Sasaki and Murakami shine in MLB and Mexico signs Marquez as football coach
summary: Japanese baseball sees returns and outstanding performances, while Mexican football bets on a historic defender for the bench.

title: Securitize, the first Spanish unicorn on Wall Street, plunges 30% after its debut
summary: The real asset tokenization company debuted with gains but quickly corrected, reflecting the volatility of the crypto finance market.

title: Certificates of deposit offer up to 4.10% APR while mortgages continue to fall
summary: Long-term interest rates continue their decline in the United States, benefiting homebuyers.

title: FCA accuses lawyer of insider trading and criticizes car lenders
summary: The British regulator intensifies market surveillance with insider trading charges and warnings about oversight in auto financing.

title: Iberdrola, BBVA and Telefónica lead green bond issuance and HSBC places the utility among the winners of the next decade
summary: The commitment to sustainable financing is consolidated in Spain, with Iberdrola highlighted for its strategic position in the energy transition.

title: China uses rare earth ban to pressure foreign companies
summary: The restrictive measure aims to squeeze foreign rivals and strengthen Beijing's control over critical technology supplies.
